_comment: Pinned cloud images for tools/cloud_smoke.ts — a base image this package
did NOT produce, which is the whole point: every other smoke validates
bytes we wrote against oracles, and this one validates that customizing
someone else's artifact leaves it intact.
Separate from appliance.lock.json on purpose. readApplianceIdentity()
folds that file's sha256 into every guest layer's cache key, so adding a
section to it would invalidate every appliance already built and every
layer already cached — for an image the appliance never touches.
Alpine publishes .sha512 and a GPG .asc for these, but no .sha256 sidecar.
`sha512` below is the published digest, verified against the sidecar at
the URL; `sha256` is over the same verified bytes and is what the fetcher
enforces, so the pin matches the rest of this repo's spelling.
The `measured` block is a property of the pinned bytes, not of Alpine:
every figure was read out of THIS file, and the smoke asserts them rather
than restating them in code. Refresh the whole entry together.
